function.name: get_datetime
function.description: Get the current date and time (server timezone).

function.name: remember
function.description: Write a stable, durably useful fact/preference about the user into long-term memory (kept across sessions, injected into later conversations). Use only for persistent information (such as long-term preferences, background, how to address the user); do not record one-off task details or temporary context. Duplicate content is ignored automatically.

fact.description: A one-sentence fact/preference to remember long-term

function.name: log_event
function.description: Append a noteworthy event/progress from this interaction to the user's "today" activity log (archived by date, viewable by the user in the account center). Use to record completed work, conclusions reached, files produced, etc.; do not record trivial chit-chat.

text.description: A one-sentence event/progress to record in today's log

function.name: read_log
function.description: Read the user's activity log for a given day (markdown). Date format YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate an arithmetic expression, supporting + - * / % ( ) and decimals.

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web and return summaries of relevant web pages. Use it to look up real-time or external information.

max_results.description: Number of results to return, default 5

function.name: list_files
function.description: List the files and subdirectories under a directory in the agent workspace.

path.description: Directory path, root is '/'

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read the text content of a file in the agent workspace. For large files, use offset/limit to read by line in pages. Output is cat -n style: every line is prefixed with its line number and a tab. When you feed text back to apply_patch, strip that "<number>\t" prefix ŌĆö a patch's context/old lines must match the file's RAW text.

path.description: File path, e.g. /notes/a.txt

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return (default reads to the end, capped at a limit)

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write/overwrite a text file in the agent workspace (intermediate directories are created automatically). Use this ONLY to create a new file or when replacing essentially all of an existing one. To change part of an existing file, use apply_patch and touch only the affected lines ŌĆö do NOT re-read a file and re-emit the whole thing just to make a small change.

path.description: File path, e.g. /out/result.py

function.name: pip_install
function.description: Install missing Python packages on demand for the cloud sandbox (binary wheels only). Once installed, run_python can import them; common libraries (python-docx/openpyxl/python-pptx/reportlab/pandas/numpy/matplotlib/Pillow, etc.) are preinstalled and need no installation. Installs are globally cached, so each package only needs to be installed once.

packages.description: List of package names, optionally with versions, e.g. ["openpyxl", "tqdm==4.66.5"]

function.name: run_python
function.description:
Execute Python 3.12 code in an isolated cloud sandbox (no network) and return stdout/stderr. Before execution the session workspace is synced into /workspace (the current directory); after execution, newly created/modified files are written back to the workspace automatically ŌĆö you can read and write existing files directly. 
ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Files are only kept if saved in the workspace: save outputs using **relative paths** (i.e. the current directory /workspace, equivalent to /mnt/data). Do not write to /tmp, HOME (~/...) or other absolute paths ŌĆö those directories are not synced back and files will be lost. 
The sandbox is pure Python (no node / pandoc / libreoffice), so generate documents directly with the preinstalled libraries: WordŌåÆpython-docx, ExcelŌåÆopenpyxl/XlsxWriter, PPTŌåÆpython-pptx, PDFŌåÆreportlab/pypdf/pdfplumber, dataŌåÆpandas/numpy, plottingŌåÆmatplotlib, imagesŌåÆPillow. If an import raises ModuleNotFoundError, call pip_install to install the missing package first, then retry.

function.name: search_files
function.description: Search file contents by regex (grep-style) under the working directory, returning file:line:content. Supports an include glob to restrict files (e.g. *.ts, src/**/*.py). Automatically skips .git/node_modules/binary files.

pattern.description: Search regular expression (JS syntax; matched per line)

include.description: Optional: filename glob filter, e.g. *.ts or src/**/*.py

function.name: glob_files
function.description: List file paths under the working directory matching a glob pattern (e.g. **/*.test.ts, src/*.py). Use this to find files; use search_files to search contents.

pattern.description: Glob pattern: ** across directories, * within a segment, ? single character, {a,b} alternation

function.name: web_fetch
function.description: Fetch the content of a public web page / text / JSON and convert it to readable text (HTML tags are stripped automatically, links are kept). Good for reading links found by web_search, documentation pages, or API responses. Only http/https public addresses.

max_chars.description: Max number of characters to return (default 20000, capped at 60000)

function.name: todo_write
function.description: Maintain this session's todo list (replaces the whole list). Write a list before planning a multi-step task; update the status after each step before continuing. status Ōłł pending | in_progress | completed; at most one item in_progress at a time.

todos.description: The complete todo list (replaces the old list in full)

function.name: todo_read
function.description: Read this session's current todo list (use it to restore context or check remaining steps).

function.name: apply_patch
function.description:
Make structured patch edits to one or more files (context-anchored, better than full overwrite / single-string replace; multiple files and multiple edits in one atomic commit). The patch parameter is a block of patch text, in the format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: relative path
@@ optional locating context (class/function name)
 unchanged context line (leading space)
-line to delete
+line to add
*** Add File: relative path
+every line of the new file (each starts with +)
*** Delete File: relative path
*** End Patch
Key points: always include a few unchanged context lines (starting with a space) around each change to locate it; use Add to create, Delete to remove, and to rename put a line "*** Move to: new path" right under Update File. The cloud workspace currently supports only Add/Update.

patch.description: Complete patch text (*** Begin Patch ŌĆ” *** End Patch)

function.name: display_file
function.description: Show a workspace file to the USER inline in the chat (NOT for you to read it). Images render as a clickable thumbnail the user can click to enlarge/zoom; other file types render as a clickable file card that opens a preview. Use this when you want the user to see a file you created or found (an image, a generated chart, a document, etc.). To read/analyze an image yourself, use view_image instead.

path.description: Workspace file path to display (relative to cwd, or absolute on local sessions).

function.name: generate_image
function.description: Generate an image from a text prompt and show it to the user inline in the chat (rendered as a clickable thumbnail they can enlarge). Use when the user asks to draw, paint, illustrate, render, or generate a picture. Write a vivid, concrete English `prompt`. After the image lands, give a one-sentence caption ŌĆö do NOT re-describe it in detail, and do NOT call display_file afterwards (it is already shown).

prompt.description: Vivid, concrete description of the image to generate.

model.description: Optional image model id override; defaults to the configured/first available image model.

function.name: amadeus_list_notes
function.description: List the user's Amadeus notes (markdown files in the vault). Optionally filter by a substring of the note path/name.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter note paths.

function.name: amadeus_read_note
function.description: Read one Amadeus note and return its markdown content (frontmatter and internal block markers stripped).

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md".

function.name: amadeus_write_note
function.description: Create or overwrite an Amadeus note with plain markdown. To edit an existing note, read it first, modify, then write the full new content. Overwriting resets the note to a simple linear layout.

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md" (".md" appended if missing).

content.description: The full markdown content of the note.

function.name: amadeus_list_calendars
function.description: List the user's calendars (multi-dimensional tables that have a calendarDate column), with their names, paths, event counts and columns.

function.name: amadeus_list_events
function.description: List calendar events/schedule. Optionally restrict to one calendar (by name or path) and/or a date range. Returns each event's calendar, id, title, start, end, allDay and other properties.

calendar.description: Optional calendar name or path to restrict to.

from.description: Optional inclusive start date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

to.description: Optional inclusive end date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

function.name: amadeus_create_event
function.description: Add an event to the user's calendar. Times are "YYYY-MM-DD" (all-day) or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm" (with a time).

end.description: Optional end, same format as start.

allDay.description: Optional; force all-day (drops any time component).

calendar.description: Optional calendar name/path; defaults to the first calendar.

props.description: Optional extra column values, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_edit_event
function.description: Edit an existing calendar event by id. Only the fields you pass are changed.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

end.description: New end, same format; pass empty string to clear.

allDay.description: Force all-day (drops time).

props.description: Extra column values to set, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_delete_event
function.description: Delete a calendar event by id.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

Make structured patch edits to one or more files (context-anchored, better than full overwrite / single-string replace; multiple files and multiple edits in one atomic commit). The patch parameter is a block of patch text, in the format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: relative path
@@ optional locating context (class/function name)
 unchanged context line (leading space)
-line to delete
+line to add
*** Add File: relative path
+every line of the new file (each starts with +)
*** Delete File: relative path
*** End Patch
Key points: always include a few unchanged context lines (starting with a space) around each change to locate it; use Add to create, Delete to remove, and to rename put a line "*** Move to: new path" right under Update File. The cloud workspace currently supports only Add/Update.

patch.description: Complete patch text (*** Begin Patch ŌĆ” *** End Patch)

function.name: display_file
function.description: Show a workspace file to the USER inline in the chat (NOT for you to read it). Images render as a clickable thumbnail the user can click to enlarge/zoom; other file types render as a clickable file card that opens a preview. Use this when you want the user to see a file you created or found (an image, a generated chart, a document, etc.). To read/analyze an image yourself, use view_image instead.

path.description: Workspace file path to display (relative to cwd, or absolute on local sessions).

function.name: generate_image
function.description: Generate an image from a text prompt and show it to the user inline in the chat (rendered as a clickable thumbnail they can enlarge). Use when the user asks to draw, paint, illustrate, render, or generate a picture. Write a vivid, concrete English `prompt`. After the image lands, give a one-sentence caption ŌĆö do NOT re-describe it in detail, and do NOT call display_file afterwards (it is already shown).

prompt.description: Vivid, concrete description of the image to generate.

model.description: Optional image model id override; defaults to the configured/first available image model.

function.name: get_datetime
function.description: Get the current date and time (server timezone).

function.name: remember
function.description: Write a stable, durably useful fact/preference about the user into long-term memory (kept across sessions, injected into later conversations). Use only for persistent information (such as long-term preferences, background, how to address the user); do not record one-off task details or temporary context. Duplicate content is ignored automatically.

fact.description: A one-sentence fact/preference to remember long-term

function.name: log_event
function.description: Append a noteworthy event/progress from this interaction to the user's "today" activity log (archived by date, viewable by the user in the account center). Use to record completed work, conclusions reached, files produced, etc.; do not record trivial chit-chat.

text.description: A one-sentence event/progress to record in today's log

function.name: read_log
function.description: Read the user's activity log for a given day (markdown). Date format YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate an arithmetic expression, supporting + - * / % ( ) and decimals.

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web and return summaries of relevant web pages. Use it to look up real-time or external information.

max_results.description: Number of results to return, default 5

function.name: list_files
function.description: List the files and subdirectories under a directory in the agent workspace.

path.description: Directory path, root is '/'

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read the text content of a file in the agent workspace. For large files, use offset/limit to read by line in pages. Output is cat -n style: every line is prefixed with its line number and a tab. When you feed text back to apply_patch, strip that "<number>\t" prefix ŌĆö a patch's context/old lines must match the file's RAW text.

path.description: File path, e.g. /notes/a.txt

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return (default reads to the end, capped at a limit)

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write/overwrite a text file in the agent workspace (intermediate directories are created automatically). Use this ONLY to create a new file or when replacing essentially all of an existing one. To change part of an existing file, use apply_patch and touch only the affected lines ŌĆö do NOT re-read a file and re-emit the whole thing just to make a small change.

path.description: File path, e.g. /out/result.py

function.name: pip_install
function.description: Install missing Python packages on demand for the cloud sandbox (binary wheels only). Once installed, run_python can import them; common libraries (python-docx/openpyxl/python-pptx/reportlab/pandas/numpy/matplotlib/Pillow, etc.) are preinstalled and need no installation. Installs are globally cached, so each package only needs to be installed once.

packages.description: List of package names, optionally with versions, e.g. ["openpyxl", "tqdm==4.66.5"]

function.name: run_python
function.description:
Execute Python 3.12 code in an isolated cloud sandbox (no network) and return stdout/stderr. Before execution the session workspace is synced into /workspace (the current directory); after execution, newly created/modified files are written back to the workspace automatically ŌĆö you can read and write existing files directly. 
ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Files are only kept if saved in the workspace: save outputs using **relative paths** (i.e. the current directory /workspace, equivalent to /mnt/data). Do not write to /tmp, HOME (~/...) or other absolute paths ŌĆö those directories are not synced back and files will be lost. 
The sandbox is pure Python (no node / pandoc / libreoffice), so generate documents directly with the preinstalled libraries: WordŌåÆpython-docx, ExcelŌåÆopenpyxl/XlsxWriter, PPTŌåÆpython-pptx, PDFŌåÆreportlab/pypdf/pdfplumber, dataŌåÆpandas/numpy, plottingŌåÆmatplotlib, imagesŌåÆPillow. If an import raises ModuleNotFoundError, call pip_install to install the missing package first, then retry.

function.name: search_files
function.description: Search file contents by regex (grep-style) under the working directory, returning file:line:content. Supports an include glob to restrict files (e.g. *.ts, src/**/*.py). Automatically skips .git/node_modules/binary files.

pattern.description: Search regular expression (JS syntax; matched per line)

include.description: Optional: filename glob filter, e.g. *.ts or src/**/*.py

function.name: glob_files
function.description: List file paths under the working directory matching a glob pattern (e.g. **/*.test.ts, src/*.py). Use this to find files; use search_files to search contents.

pattern.description: Glob pattern: ** across directories, * within a segment, ? single character, {a,b} alternation

function.name: web_fetch
function.description: Fetch the content of a public web page / text / JSON and convert it to readable text (HTML tags are stripped automatically, links are kept). Good for reading links found by web_search, documentation pages, or API responses. Only http/https public addresses.

max_chars.description: Max number of characters to return (default 20000, capped at 60000)

function.name: todo_write
function.description: Maintain this session's todo list (replaces the whole list). Write a list before planning a multi-step task; update the status after each step before continuing. status Ōłł pending | in_progress | completed; at most one item in_progress at a time.

todos.description: The complete todo list (replaces the old list in full)

function.name: todo_read
function.description: Read this session's current todo list (use it to restore context or check remaining steps).

function.name: delegate
function.description: Delegate an independent subtask to a subagent (its own context, the same set of tools, up to 8 turns) and return its final report. This is the quick, one-shot mode: fire-and-forget, no back-and-forth (for genuine multi-round deliberation with a peer, use start_discussion instead). Good for tasks with long intermediate steps such as broad searches or batch file analysis ŌĆö the process does not consume your context, you only get the conclusion. Pass agentSlug to run a specific named agent as the subagent (it takes on that agent's persona/model) ŌĆö e.g. an agent the user @-mentioned. Or pass instructions to spin up an ad-hoc subagent with custom instructions you write on the fly (no saved agent needed). The subtask description must be self-contained (the subagent cannot see the current conversation).

task.description: Subtask description (self-contained, clear goal, stating what to return)

context.description: Optional: background information the subagent needs (relevant paths, known conclusions, etc.)

agentSlug.description: Optional: delegate to a specific named agent by its slug (runs with that agent's persona). Use the slug of an agent the user @-mentioned; omit for a generic or ad-hoc subagent.

instructions.description: Optional: inline instructions/role for an ad-hoc subagent you create on the fly (used when no agentSlug is given) ŌĆö define its focus and how to work.

name.description: Optional: a short display name for the ad-hoc subagent.

function.name: ask_user
function.description: Ask the user a question and wait for an answer (optionally with candidate options; the user can also type freely). Only use this when **user decision is truly needed**: the requirement is ambiguous, multiple reasonable approaches need a trade-off, or an operation has broad impact and needs confirmation. Do not ask about things you can infer from context/code.

question.description: The full question (end with a question mark, give enough context)

options.description: Candidate options (may be empty; at most 6, one sentence each; put the recommended one first)

*** Delete File: relative path

patch.description: Complete patch text (*** Begin Patch ŌĆ” *** End Patch)

function.name: start_discussion
function.description: Spawn a background discussion: a fork of yourself debates a peer agent over several rounds until both vote to end, then a moderator synthesizes a conclusion. Returns a discussionId immediately and runs in the background (non-blocking) ŌĆö you can keep working and call wait_discussion later, or call it right away to block until the conclusion is ready. Use this (instead of delegate) when the task benefits from genuine back-and-forth deliberation with another agent rather than a one-shot subtask. Provide peer (a named agent slug, e.g. one the user @-mentioned) OR instructions (to spin up an ad-hoc peer). The topic must be self-contained (the peer cannot see this conversation).

topic.description: What to discuss / the question to deliberate (self-contained ŌĆö frame it fully; the peer cannot see your conversation).

peer.description: Optional: a named agent slug to discuss with (use the slug of an @-mentioned agent). Provide peer OR instructions.

instructions.description: Optional: inline persona/role for an ad-hoc peer when no named agent fits. Provide peer OR instructions.

context.description: Optional: background the peer needs (relevant paths, constraints, known facts).

maxRounds.description: Optional: how deep the discussion may go (rounds; default 7, max 30). Voting can end it earlier.

function.name: wait_discussion
function.description: Retrieve the conclusion of a background discussion started with start_discussion. If it has finished, returns the synthesized conclusion immediately; if it is still running, blocks until it finishes (then returns the conclusion). If it is taking too long it returns the discussion so far plus a note ŌĆö you may call it again.

function.description: Show a workspace file to the USER inline in the chat (NOT for you to read it). Images render as a clickable thumbnail the user can click to enlarge/zoom; other file types render as a clickable file card that opens a preview. Use this when you want the user to see a file you created or found (an image, a generated chart, a document, etc.). To read/analyze an image yourself, use view_image instead.

path.description: Workspace file path to display (relative to cwd, or absolute on local sessions).

function.name: inbox_send
function.description: Send a message to the user's inbox (the app's notification center). Use it for results, reminders, reports, or follow-ups the user should notice outside this conversation ŌĆö not as a substitute for replying here. Optionally set deliver_at to schedule a one-time future delivery; there is no recurring schedule.

title.description: Concise message title shown in the inbox list

body.description: Optional message body (plain text or simple markdown)

deliver_at.description: Optional one-time delivery time: ISO 8601 (e.g. "2026-07-02T18:30:00+08:00") or "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm" interpreted in the user's local timezone. Omit to deliver immediately.

function.name: amadeus_list_notes
function.description: List the user's Amadeus notes (markdown files in the vault). Optionally filter by a substring of the note path/name.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter note paths.

function.name: amadeus_read_note
function.description: Read one Amadeus note and return its markdown content (frontmatter and internal block markers stripped).

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md".

function.name: amadeus_write_note
function.description: Create or overwrite an Amadeus note with plain markdown. To edit an existing note, read it first, modify, then write the full new content. Overwriting resets the note to a simple linear layout.

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md" (".md" appended if missing).

content.description: The full markdown content of the note.

function.name: amadeus_list_calendars
function.description: List the user's calendars (multi-dimensional tables that have a calendarDate column), with their names, paths, event counts and columns.

function.name: amadeus_list_events
function.description: List calendar events/schedule. Optionally restrict to one calendar (by name or path) and/or a date range. Returns each event's calendar, id, title, start, end, allDay and other properties.

calendar.description: Optional calendar name or path to restrict to.

from.description: Optional inclusive start date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

to.description: Optional inclusive end date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

function.name: amadeus_create_event
function.description: Add an event to the user's calendar. Times are "YYYY-MM-DD" (all-day) or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm" (with a time).

end.description: Optional end, same format as start.

allDay.description: Optional; force all-day (drops any time component).

calendar.description: Optional calendar name/path; defaults to the first calendar.

props.description: Optional extra column values, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_edit_event
function.description: Edit an existing calendar event by id. Only the fields you pass are changed.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

end.description: New end, same format; pass empty string to clear.

allDay.description: Force all-day (drops time).

props.description: Extra column values to set, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_delete_event
function.description: Delete a calendar event by id.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

function.name: muse_watch
function.description: Manage watch rules for Muse, the background agent: when a rule fires, Muse wakes up and turns it into a todo/reminder for the user. Use when the user asks to "keep an eye on" something, e.g. "remind me when xxx.md reaches 100 characters" ŌåÆ set a file_chars_gte rule. Condition types: file_chars_gte (file reaches n non-whitespace chars), event_seen (a new in-app activity line contains `match`, e.g. a file name or event like "note.edit"), daily_at (fires once a day after HH:MM). Rules are evaluated by code every few minutes at zero cost; Muse only runs when one fires.

action.description: set=add a rule, list=show rules, remove=delete by id

desc.description: set: human-readable description of the task (shown to the user)

path.description: set(file_chars_gte): file path (absolute, or relative to the current workspace)

match.description: set(event_seen): substring to look for in new activity lines

prompt.description: set: optional extra instruction for Muse when the rule fires (English preferred)

cooldown_hours.description: set: hours to wait before the rule may fire again (default 24)

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md" (".md" appended if missing).

content.description: The full markdown content of the note.

function.name: amadeus_list_calendars
function.description: List the user's calendars (multi-dimensional tables that have a calendarDate column), with their names, paths, event counts and columns.

function.name: amadeus_list_events
function.description: List calendar events/schedule. Optionally restrict to one calendar (by name or path) and/or a date range. Returns each event's calendar, id, title, start, end, allDay and other properties.

calendar.description: Optional calendar name or path to restrict to.

from.description: Optional inclusive start date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

to.description: Optional inclusive end date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

function.name: amadeus_create_event
function.description: Add an event to the user's calendar. Times are "YYYY-MM-DD" (all-day) or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm" (with a time).

end.description: Optional end, same format as start.

allDay.description: Optional; force all-day (drops any time component).

calendar.description: Optional calendar name/path; defaults to the first calendar.

props.description: Optional extra column values, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_edit_event
function.description: Edit an existing calendar event by id. Only the fields you pass are changed.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

end.description: New end, same format; pass empty string to clear.

allDay.description: Force all-day (drops time).

props.description: Extra column values to set, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_delete_event
function.description: Delete a calendar event by id.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

function.name: muse_watch
function.description: Manage watch rules for Muse, the background agent: when a rule fires, Muse wakes up and turns it into a todo/reminder for the user. Use when the user asks to "keep an eye on" something, e.g. "remind me when xxx.md reaches 100 characters" ŌåÆ set a file_chars_gte rule. Condition types: file_chars_gte (file reaches n non-whitespace chars), event_seen (a new in-app activity line contains `match`, e.g. a file name or event like "note.edit"), daily_at (fires once a day after HH:MM). Rules are evaluated by code every few minutes at zero cost; Muse only runs when one fires.

action.description: set=add a rule, list=show rules, remove=delete by id

desc.description: set: human-readable description of the task (shown to the user)

path.description: set(file_chars_gte): file path (absolute, or relative to the current workspace)

match.description: set(event_seen): substring to look for in new activity lines

prompt.description: set: optional extra instruction for Muse when the rule fires (English preferred)

cooldown_hours.description: set: hours to wait before the rule may fire again (default 24)

function.name: get_datetime
function.description: Get the current date and time (server timezone).

function.name: remember
function.description: Write a stable, durably useful fact/preference about the user into long-term memory (kept across sessions, injected into later conversations). Use only for persistent information (such as long-term preferences, background, how to address the user); do not record one-off task details or temporary context. Duplicate content is ignored automatically.

fact.description: A one-sentence fact/preference to remember long-term

function.name: log_event
function.description: Append a noteworthy event/progress from this interaction to the user's "today" activity log (archived by date, viewable by the user in the account center). Use to record completed work, conclusions reached, files produced, etc.; do not record trivial chit-chat.

text.description: A one-sentence event/progress to record in today's log

function.name: read_log
function.description: Read the user's activity log for a given day (markdown). Date format YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate an arithmetic expression, supporting + - * / % ( ) and decimals.

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web and return summaries of relevant web pages. Use it to look up real-time or external information.

max_results.description: Number of results to return, default 5

function.name: run_bash
function.description: Run a single shell command (/bin/sh -c) on the user's machine, with the current session cwd as working directory; returns stdout/stderr/exit_code. Use for running builds/tests, listing directories, git operations, etc. Destructive commands may require user approval.

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read the text content of a file on the machine (path resolved relative to the current working directory). For large files, paginate by line with offset/limit. Output is cat -n style: every line is prefixed with its line number and a tab. When you later feed text to edit_file/multi_edit, strip that "<number>\t" prefix ŌĆö old_string must match the file's RAW text, not the numbered view.

path.description: File path (relative to cwd or absolute)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return (default reads to the end, capped by an upper limit)

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write/overwrite a text file on the machine (intermediate directories are created automatically, path relative to the current working directory). Use this ONLY to create a new file or when replacing essentially all of an existing one. To change part of an existing file, use edit_file / multi_edit / apply_patch and touch only the affected lines ŌĆö do NOT re-read a file and re-emit the whole thing just to make a small change.

path.description: File path (relative to cwd or absolute)

function.name: edit_file
function.description: Make an exact string replacement in a file on the machine: replace the single occurrence of old_string with new_string (indentation/whitespace must match exactly). old_string must appear **exactly once** in the file, otherwise it errors ŌĆö this keeps changes safe and controlled. Prefer this over rewriting a whole file: to change part of an existing file, edit only the affected lines. old_string matches the RAW file text ŌĆö do NOT include the leading "<number>\t" prefix that read_file shows. To create a new file, use write_file.

path.description: File path (relative to cwd or absolute)

old_string.description: The original text to replace (must match uniquely)

new_string.description: The new text to replace it with

function.name: list_dir
function.description: List the files and subdirectories under a directory on the machine (path relative to the current working directory, defaults to listing cwd itself).

path.description: Directory path (relative to cwd or absolute), defaults to the current directory

function.name: multi_edit
function.description: Make multiple exact replacements in a single file on the machine (atomic: writes only if all match, leaves the file untouched if any fails). Each edit's old_string must appear exactly once in the text after the preceding edits have been applied in order (matching the RAW text ŌĆö do NOT include read_file's "<number>\t" line prefix). Use this to change several regions of an existing file in one shot instead of rewriting the whole file; it is safer and saves turns versus firing off several edit_file calls.

path.description: File path (relative to cwd or absolute)

edits.description: List of replacements to apply in order

old_string.description: The original text to replace (must match uniquely)

new_string.description: The new text to replace it with

function.name: view_image
function.description: View an image file on the machine: provides the image as visual content for you to "see", for recognizing screenshots, analyzing charts/photos/design mockups/UI, etc. Supports png/jpg/jpeg/gif/webp/bmp; path resolved relative to the current working directory. Use this tool when you need to "look at / read an image"; do not use read_file (it only returns a text hint for images).

path.description: Image file path (relative to cwd or absolute)

function.name: read_document
function.description: Extract the text/markdown content of a document so you can actually read it ŌĆö use this instead of read_file for binary documents (read_file would return garbled bytes). PDF works out of the box; docx/xlsx/pptx need LibreOffice installed on the machine. Path resolved relative to the current working directory. Text-based PDFs need no OCR; for scanned/image-only PDFs set ocr:true (slower, fetches a small OCR model on first use).

path.description: Document file path (relative to cwd or absolute), e.g. report.pdf

ocr.description: Enable OCR for scanned/image-only PDFs (default false; off is faster and lighter)

function.name: search_files
function.description: Search file contents by regex (grep-style) under the working directory, returning file:line:content. Supports an include glob to restrict files (e.g. *.ts, src/**/*.py). Automatically skips .git/node_modules/binary files.

pattern.description: Search regular expression (JS syntax; matched per line)

include.description: Optional: filename glob filter, e.g. *.ts or src/**/*.py

function.name: glob_files
function.description: List file paths under the working directory matching a glob pattern (e.g. **/*.test.ts, src/*.py). Use this to find files; use search_files to search contents.

pattern.description: Glob pattern: ** across directories, * within a segment, ? single character, {a,b} alternation

function.name: web_fetch
function.description: Fetch the content of a public web page / text / JSON and convert it to readable text (HTML tags are stripped automatically, links are kept). Good for reading links found by web_search, documentation pages, or API responses. Only http/https public addresses.

max_chars.description: Max number of characters to return (default 20000, capped at 60000)

function.name: browser_search
function.description: Open a search engine in the local lightweight browser and return a page snapshot. Prefer this for real-time web search; the @eN refs in the results can be used with browser_click/browser_type to interact further.

engine.description: Optional search engine, defaults to the configured value

function.name: browser_navigate
function.description: Open a public http/https URL in the local lightweight browser and return the title, final URL, and a compact snapshot.

function.name: browser_snapshot
function.description: Read the accessibility-tree snapshot of the current browser page. compact=true returns a shorter view of interactive elements.

function.name: browser_click
function.description: Click an element ref from the snapshot, e.g. @e5. After clicking, typically call browser_snapshot to refresh the page state.

function.name: browser_type
function.description: Fill text into an input element ref from the snapshot; clears the existing value before typing.

function.name: browser_scroll
function.description: Scroll the current page. direction is up or down, pixels defaults to 500.

function.name: browser_back
function.description: Navigate the browser back to the previous page.

function.name: browser_press
function.description: Press a key on the current page, e.g. Enter, Tab, Escape.

function.name: browser_console
function.description: Read console/errors, or when an expression is provided, evaluate a JS expression in the page context.

expression.description: Optional JS expression; omit to read console/errors

function.name: browser_screenshot
function.description: Save a screenshot of the current page to ~/.tangu/browser/screenshots and return the screenshot_path.

function.name: todo_write
function.description: Maintain this session's todo list (replaces the whole list). Write a list before planning a multi-step task; update the status after each step before continuing. status Ōłł pending | in_progress | completed; at most one item in_progress at a time.

todos.description: The complete todo list (replaces the old list in full)

function.name: todo_read
function.description: Read this session's current todo list (use it to restore context or check remaining steps).

function.name: run_background
function.description: Start a long-running shell command in the background on this machine (dev server, watch, long test, etc.) and return a process_id immediately. Use read_process_output to view output, list_processes to check status, kill_process to terminate. Use run_bash for one-off commands; only use this for things that must keep running or must not block subsequent steps. You can also start **interactive** processes (such as `python3 -i`, `node`, a question-and-answer CLI), then feed input to their stdin with write_process_input. Note: this is a pipe, not a real TTY ŌĆö it supports line-based input (REPL/debugger/line-by-line prompts), but not full-screen TUIs (vim/top), programs that only become interactive when isatty is detected, or reading passwords from /dev/tty (sudo/ssh).

command.description: The shell command to run in the background

function.name: list_processes
function.description: List the background processes started in this session (id/command/status/uptime).

function.name: read_process_output
function.description: Read the accumulated output of a background process (stdout+stderr merged). offset is a character offset; omit it to read the tail.

process_id.description: The process id returned by run_background

offset.description: Starting character offset (defaults to the most recent 20000 characters)

function.name: kill_process
function.description: Terminate a background process (SIGTERMŌåÆ3sŌåÆSIGKILL).

process_id.description: The id of the process to terminate

function.name: write_process_input
function.description: Write one line of input to a background process's stdin to drive an interactive process (REPL/debugger/Q&A CLI). A trailing newline is appended by default (most line-based programs need a newline to process input). After writing, it waits for the process to produce new output and settle, then returns the **new** output. Leave input empty to only poll (don't write, just see what else the process emitted); pass a single Ctrl-C character as input to send an interrupt signal. The process must have been started by run_background and still be running.

process_id.description: The process id returned by run_background

input.description: The text to write to stdin (leave empty to only poll for new output)

append_newline.description: Whether to append a trailing newline (default true)

yield_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds to wait for new output (default 8000, range 250-15000)

function.name: delegate
function.description: Delegate an independent subtask to a subagent (its own context, the same set of tools, up to 8 turns) and return its final report. This is the quick, one-shot mode: fire-and-forget, no back-and-forth (for genuine multi-round deliberation with a peer, use start_discussion instead). Good for tasks with long intermediate steps such as broad searches or batch file analysis ŌĆö the process does not consume your context, you only get the conclusion. Pass agentSlug to run a specific named agent as the subagent (it takes on that agent's persona/model) ŌĆö e.g. an agent the user @-mentioned. Or pass instructions to spin up an ad-hoc subagent with custom instructions you write on the fly (no saved agent needed). The subtask description must be self-contained (the subagent cannot see the current conversation).

task.description: Subtask description (self-contained, clear goal, stating what to return)

context.description: Optional: background information the subagent needs (relevant paths, known conclusions, etc.)

agentSlug.description: Optional: delegate to a specific named agent by its slug (runs with that agent's persona). Use the slug of an agent the user @-mentioned; omit for a generic or ad-hoc subagent.

instructions.description: Optional: inline instructions/role for an ad-hoc subagent you create on the fly (used when no agentSlug is given) ŌĆö define its focus and how to work.

name.description: Optional: a short display name for the ad-hoc subagent.

function.name: ask_user
function.description: Ask the user a question and wait for an answer (optionally with candidate options; the user can also type freely). Only use this when **user decision is truly needed**: the requirement is ambiguous, multiple reasonable approaches need a trade-off, or an operation has broad impact and needs confirmation. Do not ask about things you can infer from context/code.

question.description: The full question (end with a question mark, give enough context)

options.description: Candidate options (may be empty; at most 6, one sentence each; put the recommended one first)

function.name: manage_agent
function.description: Create/update/delete/list local "Normal Agents" (a reusable conversational persona = system prompt + model + tools + settings). When you discover a role/way of working worth reusing, use action="create" to capture it as an agent for the user to select later. action Ōłł create | update | delete | list. create/update require name and system_prompt.

slug.description: Unique agent identifier (lowercase alphanumerics and hyphens); required for update/delete, optional for create (derived from name)

system_prompt.description: The agent's development instructions (what to do / how to do it / what to read; required for create/update)

model.description: Model id that overrides the session model (optional)

tools.description: Allowlist of enabled custom/MCP tool ids (optional)

function.name: wechat_send_file
function.description: Send a file from the workspace as a "file" to the WeChat user connected to the current WeChat session. Only works in a "WeChat Remote" session (WeChat connected via QR scan); calling it in a normal session returns "WeChat not connected". path is relative to the working directory or an absolute path; any type (document/archive/audio etc.), up to 50MB. To display an image inline, use wechat_send_image instead.

path.description: Path of the file to send (relative to the working directory or an absolute path)

function.name: wechat_send_image
function.description: Send an image from the workspace inline as an "image" bubble to the WeChat user connected to the current WeChat session. Only works in a "WeChat Remote" session. path is relative to the working directory or an absolute path; good for screenshots/charts/generated images (png/jpg/gif etc.), up to 50MB. For non-images or to keep the original filename, use wechat_send_file.

path.description: Path of the image to send (png/jpg/gif etc.)

function.name: apply_patch
function.description:
Make structured patch edits to one or more files (context-anchored, better than full overwrite / single-string replace; multiple files and multiple edits in one atomic commit). The patch parameter is a block of patch text, in the format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: relative path
@@ optional locating context (class/function name)
 unchanged context line (leading space)
-line to delete
+line to add
*** Add File: relative path
+every line of the new file (each starts with +)
*** Delete File: relative path
*** End Patch
Key points: always include a few unchanged context lines (starting with a space) around each change to locate it; use Add to create, Delete to remove, and to rename put a line "*** Move to: new path" right under Update File. The cloud workspace currently supports only Add/Update.

patch.description: Complete patch text (*** Begin Patch ŌĆ” *** End Patch)

function.name: start_discussion
function.description: Spawn a background discussion: a fork of yourself debates a peer agent over several rounds until both vote to end, then a moderator synthesizes a conclusion. Returns a discussionId immediately and runs in the background (non-blocking) ŌĆö you can keep working and call wait_discussion later, or call it right away to block until the conclusion is ready. Use this (instead of delegate) when the task benefits from genuine back-and-forth deliberation with another agent rather than a one-shot subtask. Provide peer (a named agent slug, e.g. one the user @-mentioned) OR instructions (to spin up an ad-hoc peer). The topic must be self-contained (the peer cannot see this conversation).

topic.description: What to discuss / the question to deliberate (self-contained ŌĆö frame it fully; the peer cannot see your conversation).

peer.description: Optional: a named agent slug to discuss with (use the slug of an @-mentioned agent). Provide peer OR instructions.

instructions.description: Optional: inline persona/role for an ad-hoc peer when no named agent fits. Provide peer OR instructions.

context.description: Optional: background the peer needs (relevant paths, constraints, known facts).

maxRounds.description: Optional: how deep the discussion may go (rounds; default 7, max 30). Voting can end it earlier.

function.name: wait_discussion
function.description: Retrieve the conclusion of a background discussion started with start_discussion. If it has finished, returns the synthesized conclusion immediately; if it is still running, blocks until it finishes (then returns the conclusion). If it is taking too long it returns the discussion so far plus a note ŌĆö you may call it again.

function.name: display_file
function.description: Show a workspace file to the USER inline in the chat (NOT for you to read it). Images render as a clickable thumbnail the user can click to enlarge/zoom; other file types render as a clickable file card that opens a preview. Use this when you want the user to see a file you created or found (an image, a generated chart, a document, etc.). To read/analyze an image yourself, use view_image instead.

path.description: Workspace file path to display (relative to cwd, or absolute on local sessions).

function.name: generate_image
function.description: Generate an image from a text prompt and show it to the user inline in the chat (rendered as a clickable thumbnail they can enlarge). Use when the user asks to draw, paint, illustrate, render, or generate a picture. Write a vivid, concrete English `prompt`. After the image lands, give a one-sentence caption ŌĆö do NOT re-describe it in detail, and do NOT call display_file afterwards (it is already shown).

prompt.description: Vivid, concrete description of the image to generate.

model.description: Optional image model id override; defaults to the configured/first available image model.

function.name: inbox_send
function.description: Send a message to the user's inbox (the app's notification center). Use it for results, reminders, reports, or follow-ups the user should notice outside this conversation ŌĆö not as a substitute for replying here. Optionally set deliver_at to schedule a one-time future delivery; there is no recurring schedule.

title.description: Concise message title shown in the inbox list

body.description: Optional message body (plain text or simple markdown)

deliver_at.description: Optional one-time delivery time: ISO 8601 (e.g. "2026-07-02T18:30:00+08:00") or "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm" interpreted in the user's local timezone. Omit to deliver immediately.

function.name: browser_task
function.description: Delegate a whole multi-step web task to an autonomous browser agent (browser-use) that drives the user's real, logged-in local Chrome with vision + reasoning, and returns the final result. Use this for goals that need several page interactions or the user's existing logins ŌĆö e.g. "check my inbox for X", "book the cheapest flight on <site>", "fill and submit this form", "research <topic> across these pages". It runs its own loop end-to-end (no step-by-step driving from you). For a single navigate/click/read, prefer the lighter browser_navigate/browser_click tools instead. It acts as the user on logged-in sites ŌĆö scope it with allowed_domains when possible.

task.description: The full natural-language goal to accomplish in the browser.

start_url.description: Optional URL to open first before pursuing the task.

max_steps.description: Optional cap on the agent's browser steps (default 50).

allowed_domains.description: Optional domain allowlist to scope where the agent may navigate (e.g. ["*.github.com"]).

function.name: amadeus_list_notes
function.description: List the user's Amadeus notes (markdown files in the vault). Optionally filter by a substring of the note path/name.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter note paths.

function.name: amadeus_read_note
function.description: Read one Amadeus note and return its markdown content (frontmatter and internal block markers stripped).

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md".

content.description: The full markdown content of the note.

To create a new file, use write_file.

path.description: File path (relative to cwd or absolute)

old_string.description: The original text to replace (must match uniquely)

new_string.description: The new text to replace it with

function.name: list_dir
function.description: List the files and subdirectories under a directory on the machine (path relative to the current working directory, defaults to listing cwd itself).

path.description: Directory path (relative to cwd or absolute), defaults to the current directory

function.name: multi_edit
function.description: Make multiple exact replacements in a single file on the machine (atomic: writes only if all match, leaves the file untouched if any fails). Each edit's old_string must appear exactly once in the text after the preceding edits have been applied in order (matching the RAW text ŌĆö do NOT include read_file's "<number>\t" line prefix). Use this to change several regions of an existing file in one shot instead of rewriting the whole file; it is safer and saves turns versus firing off several edit_file calls.

path.description: File path (relative to cwd or absolute)

edits.description: List of replacements to apply in order

old_string.description: The original text to replace (must match uniquely)

new_string.description: The new text to replace it with

function.name: view_image
function.description: View an image file on the machine: provides the image as visual content for you to "see", for recognizing screenshots, analyzing charts/photos/design mockups/UI, etc. Supports png/jpg/jpeg/gif/webp/bmp; path resolved relative to the current working directory. Use this tool when you need to "look at / read an image"; do not use read_file (it only returns a text hint for images).

path.description: Image file path (relative to cwd or absolute)

function.name: read_document
function.description: Extract the text/markdown content of a document so you can actually read it ŌĆö use this instead of read_file for binary documents (read_file would return garbled bytes). PDF works out of the box; docx/xlsx/pptx need LibreOffice installed on the machine. Path resolved relative to the current working directory. Text-based PDFs need no OCR; for scanned/image-only PDFs set ocr:true (slower, fetches a small OCR model on first use).

path.description: Document file path (relative to cwd or absolute), e.g. report.pdf

ocr.description: Enable OCR for scanned/image-only PDFs (default false; off is faster and lighter)

function.name: search_files
function.description: Search file contents by regex (grep-style) under the working directory, returning file:line:content. Supports an include glob to restrict files (e.g. *.ts, src/**/*.py). Automatically skips .git/node_modules/binary files.

pattern.description: Search regular expression (JS syntax; matched per line)

include.description: Optional: filename glob filter, e.g. *.ts or src/**/*.py

function.name: glob_files
function.description: List file paths under the working directory matching a glob pattern (e.g. **/*.test.ts, src/*.py). Use this to find files; use search_files to search contents.

pattern.description: Glob pattern: ** across directories, * within a segment, ? single character, {a,b} alternation

function.name: web_fetch
function.description: Fetch the content of a public web page / text / JSON and convert it to readable text (HTML tags are stripped automatically, links are kept). Good for reading links found by web_search, documentation pages, or API responses. Only http/https public addresses.

max_chars.description: Max number of characters to return (default 20000, capped at 60000)

function.name: browser_search
function.description: Open a search engine in the local lightweight browser and return a page snapshot. Prefer this for real-time web search; the @eN refs in the results can be used with browser_click/browser_type to interact further.

engine.description: Optional search engine, defaults to the configured value

function.name: browser_navigate
function.description: Open a public http/https URL in the local lightweight browser and return the title, final URL, and a compact snapshot.

function.name: browser_snapshot
function.description: Read the accessibility-tree snapshot of the current browser page. compact=true returns a shorter view of interactive elements.

function.name: browser_click
function.description: Click an element ref from the snapshot, e.g. @e5. After clicking, typically call browser_snapshot to refresh the page state.

function.name: browser_type
function.description: Fill text into an input element ref from the snapshot; clears the existing value before typing.

function.name: browser_scroll
function.description: Scroll the current page. direction is up or down, pixels defaults to 500.

function.name: browser_back
function.description: Navigate the browser back to the previous page.

function.name: browser_press
function.description: Press a key on the current page, e.g. Enter, Tab, Escape.

function.name: browser_console
function.description: Read console/errors, or when an expression is provided, evaluate a JS expression in the page context.

expression.description: Optional JS expression; omit to read console/errors

function.name: browser_screenshot
function.description: Save a screenshot of the current page to ~/.tangu/browser/screenshots and return the screenshot_path.

function.name: todo_write
function.description: Maintain this session's todo list (replaces the whole list). Write a list before planning a multi-step task; update the status after each step before continuing. status Ōłł pending | in_progress | completed; at most one item in_progress at a time.

todos.description: The complete todo list (replaces the old list in full)

function.name: todo_read
function.description: Read this session's current todo list (use it to restore context or check remaining steps).

function.name: run_background
function.description: Start a long-running shell command in the background on this machine (dev server, watch, long test, etc.) and return a process_id immediately. Use read_process_output to view output, list_processes to check status, kill_process to terminate. Use run_bash for one-off commands; only use this for things that must keep running or must not block subsequent steps. You can also start **interactive** processes (such as `python3 -i`, `node`, a question-and-answer CLI), then feed input to their stdin with write_process_input. Note: this is a pipe, not a real TTY ŌĆö it supports line-based input (REPL/debugger/line-by-line prompts), but not full-screen TUIs (vim/top), programs that only become interactive when isatty is detected, or reading passwords from /dev/tty (sudo/ssh).

command.description: The shell command to run in the background

function.name: list_processes
function.description: List the background processes started in this session (id/command/status/uptime).

function.name: read_process_output
function.description: Read the accumulated output of a background process (stdout+stderr merged). offset is a character offset; omit it to read the tail.

process_id.description: The process id returned by run_background

offset.description: Starting character offset (defaults to the most recent 20000 characters)

function.name: kill_process
function.description: Terminate a background process (SIGTERMŌåÆ3sŌåÆSIGKILL).

process_id.description: The id of the process to terminate

function.name: write_process_input
function.description: Write one line of input to a background process's stdin to drive an interactive process (REPL/debugger/Q&A CLI). A trailing newline is appended by default (most line-based programs need a newline to process input). After writing, it waits for the process to produce new output and settle, then returns the **new** output. Leave input empty to only poll (don't write, just see what else the process emitted); pass a single Ctrl-C character as input to send an interrupt signal. The process must have been started by run_background and still be running.

process_id.description: The process id returned by run_background

input.description: The text to write to stdin (leave empty to only poll for new output)

append_newline.description: Whether to append a trailing newline (default true)

yield_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds to wait for new output (default 8000, range 250-15000)

function.name: delegate
function.description: Delegate an independent subtask to a subagent (its own context, the same set of tools, up to 8 turns) and return its final report. This is the quick, one-shot mode: fire-and-forget, no back-and-forth (for genuine multi-round deliberation with a peer, use start_discussion instead). Good for tasks with long intermediate steps such as broad searches or batch file analysis ŌĆö the process does not consume your context, you only get the conclusion. Pass agentSlug to run a specific named agent as the subagent (it takes on that agent's persona/model) ŌĆö e.g. an agent the user @-mentioned. Or pass instructions to spin up an ad-hoc subagent with custom instructions you write on the fly (no saved agent needed). The subtask description must be self-contained (the subagent cannot see the current conversation).

task.description: Subtask description (self-contained, clear goal, stating what to return)

context.description: Optional: background information the subagent needs (relevant paths, known conclusions, etc.)

agentSlug.description: Optional: delegate to a specific named agent by its slug (runs with that agent's persona). Use the slug of an agent the user @-mentioned; omit for a generic or ad-hoc subagent.

instructions.description: Optional: inline instructions/role for an ad-hoc subagent you create on the fly (used when no agentSlug is given) ŌĆö define its focus and how to work.

name.description: Optional: a short display name for the ad-hoc subagent.

function.name: ask_user
function.description: Ask the user a question and wait for an answer (optionally with candidate options; the user can also type freely). Only use this when **user decision is truly needed**: the requirement is ambiguous, multiple reasonable approaches need a trade-off, or an operation has broad impact and needs confirmation. Do not ask about things you can infer from context/code.

question.description: The full question (end with a question mark, give enough context)

options.description: Candidate options (may be empty; at most 6, one sentence each; put the recommended one first)

function.name: manage_agent
function.description: Create/update/delete/list local "Normal Agents" (a reusable conversational persona = system prompt + model + tools + settings). When you discover a role/way of working worth reusing, use action="create" to capture it as an agent for the user to select later. action Ōłł create | update | delete | list. create/update require name and system_prompt.

slug.description: Unique agent identifier (lowercase alphanumerics and hyphens); required for update/delete, optional for create (derived from name)

system_prompt.description: The agent's development instructions (what to do / how to do it / what to read; required for create/update)

model.description: Model id that overrides the session model (optional)

tools.description: Allowlist of enabled custom/MCP tool ids (optional)

function.name: wechat_send_file
function.description: Send a file from the workspace as a "file" to the WeChat user connected to the current WeChat session. Only works in a "WeChat Remote" session (WeChat connected via QR scan); calling it in a normal session returns "WeChat not connected". path is relative to the working directory or an absolute path; any type (document/archive/audio etc.), up to 50MB. To display an image inline, use wechat_send_image instead.

path.description: Path of the file to send (relative to the working directory or an absolute path)

function.name: wechat_send_image
function.description: Send an image from the workspace inline as an "image" bubble to the WeChat user connected to the current WeChat session. Only works in a "WeChat Remote" session. path is relative to the working directory or an absolute path; good for screenshots/charts/generated images (png/jpg/gif etc.), up to 50MB. For non-images or to keep the original filename, use wechat_send_file.

path.description: Path of the image to send (png/jpg/gif etc.)

function.name: apply_patch
function.description:
Make structured patch edits to one or more files (context-anchored, better than full overwrite / single-string replace; multiple files and multiple edits in one atomic commit). The patch parameter is a block of patch text, in the format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: relative path
@@ optional locating context (class/function name)
 unchanged context line (leading space)
-line to delete
+line to add
*** Add File: relative path
+every line of the new file (each starts with +)
*** Delete File: relative path
*** End Patch
Key points: always include a few unchanged context lines (starting with a space) around each change to locate it; use Add to create, Delete to remove, and to rename put a line "*** Move to: new path" right under Update File. The cloud workspace currently supports only Add/Update.

patch.description: Complete patch text (*** Begin Patch ŌĆ” *** End Patch)

function.name: start_discussion
function.description: Spawn a background discussion: a fork of yourself debates a peer agent over several rounds until both vote to end, then a moderator synthesizes a conclusion. Returns a discussionId immediately and runs in the background (non-blocking) ŌĆö you can keep working and call wait_discussion later, or call it right away to block until the conclusion is ready. Use this (instead of delegate) when the task benefits from genuine back-and-forth deliberation with another agent rather than a one-shot subtask. Provide peer (a named agent slug, e.g. one the user @-mentioned) OR instructions (to spin up an ad-hoc peer). The topic must be self-contained (the peer cannot see this conversation).

topic.description: What to discuss / the question to deliberate (self-contained ŌĆö frame it fully; the peer cannot see your conversation).

peer.description: Optional: a named agent slug to discuss with (use the slug of an @-mentioned agent). Provide peer OR instructions.

instructions.description: Optional: inline persona/role for an ad-hoc peer when no named agent fits. Provide peer OR instructions.

context.description: Optional: background the peer needs (relevant paths, constraints, known facts).

maxRounds.description: Optional: how deep the discussion may go (rounds; default 7, max 30). Voting can end it earlier.

function.name: wait_discussion
function.description: Retrieve the conclusion of a background discussion started with start_discussion. If it has finished, returns the synthesized conclusion immediately; if it is still running, blocks until it finishes (then returns the conclusion). If it is taking too long it returns the discussion so far plus a note ŌĆö you may call it again.

function.name: display_file
function.description: Show a workspace file to the USER inline in the chat (NOT for you to read it). Images render as a clickable thumbnail the user can click to enlarge/zoom; other file types render as a clickable file card that opens a preview. Use this when you want the user to see a file you created or found (an image, a generated chart, a document, etc.). To read/analyze an image yourself, use view_image instead.

path.description: Workspace file path to display (relative to cwd, or absolute on local sessions).

function.name: generate_image
function.description: Generate an image from a text prompt and show it to the user inline in the chat (rendered as a clickable thumbnail they can enlarge). Use when the user asks to draw, paint, illustrate, render, or generate a picture. Write a vivid, concrete English `prompt`. After the image lands, give a one-sentence caption ŌĆö do NOT re-describe it in detail, and do NOT call display_file afterwards (it is already shown).

prompt.description: Vivid, concrete description of the image to generate.

model.description: Optional image model id override; defaults to the configured/first available image model.

function.name: inbox_send
function.description: Send a message to the user's inbox (the app's notification center). Use it for results, reminders, reports, or follow-ups the user should notice outside this conversation ŌĆö not as a substitute for replying here. Optionally set deliver_at to schedule a one-time future delivery; there is no recurring schedule.

title.description: Concise message title shown in the inbox list

body.description: Optional message body (plain text or simple markdown)

deliver_at.description: Optional one-time delivery time: ISO 8601 (e.g. "2026-07-02T18:30:00+08:00") or "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm" interpreted in the user's local timezone. Omit to deliver immediately.

function.name: browser_task
function.description: Delegate a whole multi-step web task to an autonomous browser agent (browser-use) that drives the user's real, logged-in local Chrome with vision + reasoning, and returns the final result. Use this for goals that need several page interactions or the user's existing logins ŌĆö e.g. "check my inbox for X", "book the cheapest flight on <site>", "fill and submit this form", "research <topic> across these pages". It runs its own loop end-to-end (no step-by-step driving from you). For a single navigate/click/read, prefer the lighter browser_navigate/browser_click tools instead. It acts as the user on logged-in sites ŌĆö scope it with allowed_domains when possible.

task.description: The full natural-language goal to accomplish in the browser.

start_url.description: Optional URL to open first before pursuing the task.

max_steps.description: Optional cap on the agent's browser steps (default 50).

allowed_domains.description: Optional domain allowlist to scope where the agent may navigate (e.g. ["*.github.com"]).

function.name: amadeus_list_notes
function.description: List the user's Amadeus notes (markdown files in the vault). Optionally filter by a substring of the note path/name.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter note paths.

function.name: amadeus_read_note
function.description: Read one Amadeus note and return its markdown content (frontmatter and internal block markers stripped).

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md".

function.name: amadeus_write_note
function.description: Create or overwrite an Amadeus note with plain markdown. To edit an existing note, read it first, modify, then write the full new content. Overwriting resets the note to a simple linear layout.

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md" (".md" appended if missing).

content.description: The full markdown content of the note.

function.name: amadeus_list_calendars
function.description: List the user's calendars (multi-dimensional tables that have a calendarDate column), with their names, paths, event counts and columns.

function.name: amadeus_list_events
function.description: List calendar events/schedule. Optionally restrict to one calendar (by name or path) and/or a date range. Returns each event's calendar, id, title, start, end, allDay and other properties.

calendar.description: Optional calendar name or path to restrict to.

from.description: Optional inclusive start date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

to.description: Optional inclusive end date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

function.name: amadeus_create_event
function.description: Add an event to the user's calendar. Times are "YYYY-MM-DD" (all-day) or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm" (with a time).

end.description: Optional end, same format as start.

allDay.description: Optional; force all-day (drops any time component).

calendar.description: Optional calendar name/path; defaults to the first calendar.

props.description: Optional extra column values, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_edit_event
function.description: Edit an existing calendar event by id. Only the fields you pass are changed.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

end.description: New end, same format; pass empty string to clear.

allDay.description: Force all-day (drops time).

props.description: Extra column values to set, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_delete_event
function.description: Delete a calendar event by id.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

function.name: muse_watch
function.description: Manage watch rules for Muse, the background agent: when a rule fires, Muse wakes up and turns it into a todo/reminder for the user. Use when the user asks to "keep an eye on" something, e.g. "remind me when xxx.md reaches 100 characters" ŌåÆ set a file_chars_gte rule. Condition types: file_chars_gte (file reaches n non-whitespace chars), event_seen (a new in-app activity line contains `match`, e.g. a file name or event like "note.edit"), daily_at (fires once a day after HH:MM). desc.description: set: human-readable description of the task (shown to the user)

match.description: set(event_seen): substring to look for in new activity lines

prompt.description: set: optional extra instruction for Muse when the rule fires (English preferred)

cooldown_hours.description: set: hours to wait before the rule may fire again (default 24)

function.name: get_datetime
function.description: Get the current date and time (server timezone).

Make structured patch edits to one or more files (context-anchored, better than full overwrite / single-string replace; multiple files and multiple edits in one atomic commit). The patch parameter is a block of patch text, in the format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: relative path
@@ optional locating context (class/function name)

-line to delete
+line to add
*** Add File: relative path
+every line of the new file (each starts with +)
*** Delete File: relative path
*** End Patch
Key points: always include a few unchanged context lines (starting with a space) around each change to locate it; use Add to create, Delete to remove, and to rename put a line "*** Move to: new path" right under Update File. The cloud workspace currently supports only Add/Update.

Use run_bash for one-off commands; only use this for things that must keep running or must not block subsequent steps. You can also start **interactive** processes (such as `python3 -i`, `node`, a question-and-answer CLI), then feed input to their stdin with write_process_input. function.name: apply_patch

function.name: amadeus_list_notes
function.description: List the user's Amadeus notes (markdown files in the vault). Optionally filter by a substring of the note path/name.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter note paths.
