Images render as a clickable thumbnail the user can click to enlarge/zoom; other file types render as a clickable file card that opens a preview. Use this when you want the user to see a file you created or found (an image, a generated chart, a document, etc.). To read/analyze an image yourself, use view_image instead.

path.description: Workspace file path to display (relative to cwd, or absolute on local sessions).

function.name: generate_image
function.description: Generate an image from a text prompt and show it to the user inline in the chat (rendered as a clickable thumbnail they can enlarge). Use when the user asks to draw, paint, illustrate, render, or generate a picture. Write a vivid, concrete English `prompt`. After the image lands, give a one-sentence caption ŌĆö do NOT re-describe it in detail, and do NOT call display_file afterwards (it is already shown).

prompt.description: Vivid, concrete description of the image to generate.

model.description: Optional image model id override; defaults to the configured/first available image model.

function.name: inbox_send
function.description: Send a message to the user's inbox (the app's notification center). Use it for results, reminders, reports, or follow-ups the user should notice outside this conversation ŌĆö not as a substitute for replying here. Optionally set deliver_at to schedule a one-time future delivery; there is no recurring schedule.

title.description: Concise message title shown in the inbox list

body.description: Optional message body (plain text or simple markdown)

deliver_at.description: Optional one-time delivery time: ISO 8601 (e.g. "2026-07-02T18:30:00+08:00") or "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm" interpreted in the user's local timezone. Omit to deliver immediately.

function.name: browser_task
function.description: Delegate a whole multi-step web task to an autonomous browser agent (browser-use) that drives the user's real, logged-in local Chrome with vision + reasoning, and returns the final result. Use this for goals that need several page interactions or the user's existing logins ŌĆö e.g. "check my inbox for X", "book the cheapest flight on <site>", "fill and submit this form", "research <topic> across these pages". It runs its own loop end-to-end (no step-by-step driving from you). For a single navigate/click/read, prefer the lighter browser_navigate/browser_click tools instead. It acts as the user on logged-in sites ŌĆö scope it with allowed_domains when possible.

task.description: The full natural-language goal to accomplish in the browser.

start_url.description: Optional URL to open first before pursuing the task.

max_steps.description: Optional cap on the agent's browser steps (default 50).

allowed_domains.description: Optional domain allowlist to scope where the agent may navigate (e.g. ["*.github.com"]).

function.name: amadeus_list_notes
function.description: List the user's Amadeus notes (markdown files in the vault). Optionally filter by a substring of the note path/name.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring to filter note paths.

function.name: amadeus_read_note
function.description: Read one Amadeus note and return its markdown content (frontmatter and internal block markers stripped).

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md".

function.name: amadeus_write_note
function.description: Create or overwrite an Amadeus note with plain markdown. To edit an existing note, read it first, modify, then write the full new content. Overwriting resets the note to a simple linear layout.

path.description: Vault-relative note path, e.g. "Notes/ideas.md" (".md" appended if missing).

content.description: The full markdown content of the note.

function.name: amadeus_list_calendars
function.description: List the user's calendars (multi-dimensional tables that have a calendarDate column), with their names, paths, event counts and columns.

function.name: amadeus_list_events
function.description: List calendar events/schedule. Optionally restrict to one calendar (by name or path) and/or a date range. Returns each event's calendar, id, title, start, end, allDay and other properties.

calendar.description: Optional calendar name or path to restrict to.

from.description: Optional inclusive start date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

to.description: Optional inclusive end date filter, YYYY-MM-DD.

function.name: amadeus_create_event
function.description: Add an event to the user's calendar. Times are "YYYY-MM-DD" (all-day) or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm" (with a time).

end.description: Optional end, same format as start.

allDay.description: Optional; force all-day (drops any time component).

calendar.description: Optional calendar name/path; defaults to the first calendar.

props.description: Optional extra column values, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_edit_event
function.description: Edit an existing calendar event by id. Only the fields you pass are changed.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

end.description: New end, same format; pass empty string to clear.

allDay.description: Force all-day (drops time).

props.description: Extra column values to set, keyed by column name.

function.name: amadeus_delete_event
function.description: Delete a calendar event by id.

calendar.description: Calendar name or path the event is in.

function.name: muse_watch
function.description: Manage watch rules for Muse, the background agent: when a rule fires, Muse wakes up and turns it into a todo/reminder for the user. Use when the user asks to "keep an eye on" something, e.g. "remind me when xxx.md reaches 100 characters" ŌåÆ set a file_chars_gte rule. Condition types: file_chars_gte (file reaches n non-whitespace chars), event_seen (a new in-app activity line contains `match`, e.g. a file name or event like "note.edit"), daily_at (fires once a day after HH:MM). Rules are evaluated by code every few minutes at zero cost; Muse only runs when one fires.

action.description: set=add a rule, list=show rules, remove=delete by id

desc.description: set: human-readable description of the task (shown to the user)

path.description: set(file_chars_gte): file path (absolute, or relative to the current workspace)

match.description: set(event_seen): substring to look for in new activity lines

prompt.description: set: optional extra instruction for Muse when the rule fires (English preferred)

cooldown_hours.description: set: hours to wait before the rule may fire again (default 24)
